steps.title: Interpret the multiplier
steps.content:
In the model
$$P=P_0(2+r)^y$$
the yearly multiplier is $2+r$.

steps.title: Set the decrease condition
steps.content:
For a fixed percent decrease, the multiplier must be positive and less than $1$. This makes each year's population smaller while keeping the model positive.
$$0\lt2+r\lt1$$

steps.title: Solve the compound inequality
steps.content: Subtract $2$ from all three parts. The result is the interval where the multiplier is between $0$ and $1$. $$-2\lt r\lt -1$$ The answer is B.
